title: Content-unit schema + provenance/review-log + risk-tier router & high-risk expert-gate rules
context: repair-cafe-kits requires a structured, versioned content schema that captures every unit's citations, source licenses, review status, expert sign-off (for high-risk electrical content), and disclaimer fields. A risk-tier router classifies each unit at build time and blocks publish of any unit that violates provenance, license, no-self-approval, or (for high-risk units) expert-gate rules. This schema is provisional until the content-format ADR (arch-001) is decided; this task must not be finalized before that ADR lands.
objective: Define the RepairUnit schema (all required fields including riskClass, sources with reusePermitted, reviewedBy, approvedBy, expertSignOff, disclaimers, contentLicense, lastReviewed), the review-log format, and the risk-tier router rules that enforce citation/license validity, no-self-approval, and the high-risk expert gate at build time.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema captures all required fields: id, kind, localized title, riskClass, category, localized body, sources[] (org/title/url/retrievedDate/sourceLicense/reusePermitted), reviewStatus, reviewedBy, approvedBy (must differ), optional expertSignOff (name/credential/scopeConfirmed/date/logRef), disclaimers[], lang, contentLicense, contentVersion, lastReviewed

Risk-tier router classifies each unit as low/medium/high and the build rejects: a unit missing required citations; a unit citing a source whose sourceLicense does not permit the reuse (reusePermitted=false); a unit where approvedBy == reviewedBy

The build rejects any high-risk (riskClass=high) unit that lacks a valid expertSignOff record or is missing the mandatory 'informational, not a substitute for professional electrical training, inspection, or repair' disclaimer
Review-log format is PR-tied and append-only, capturing PR number, commit SHA, unit contentVersion, reviewer + approver, expert credential for high-risk units, and sources + licenses checked
Schema follows the content format selected in arch-001 (this task is finalized after that ADR); the schema file is committed to the repo and the risk-tier router has passing unit tests
output: A committed content-unit schema definition, review-log format specification, and risk-tier router implementation (with unit tests) that enforce citation validity, license/reusePermitted gate, no-self-approval, and the high-risk expert gate at build time.
